mango.name: Sapburn
mango.cause: Chemical injury from sap (exudate) leaking from the stem after harvest and burning the skin.
mango.symptoms: Dark-brown to black spots and blotches, often appearing as streaks or 'runs' down the side of the fruit.
mango.photo_guide: Look for glossy, dark streaks starting from the stem end. Unlike rot, the skin remains firm.

mango.name: Anthracnose
mango.cause: Fungal infection (Colletotrichum) that stays hidden in unripe fruit and develops as the fruit ripens.
mango.symptoms: Small, irregular black spots that can merge into large sunken lesions. May show pink spores in advanced stages.
mango.photo_guide: Small 'pepper spots' on green fruit that turn into large, black sunken pits on ripe fruit.
mango.action: Fruit with deep lesions should be discarded; minor spots are edible but reduce shelf life.

banana.name: Mechanical Bruising
banana.cause: Impact or compression during transport or poor post-harvest handling.
banana.symptoms: Flat, sunken, or partially broken areas of the peel that darken as the fruit ripens.
banana.photo_guide: Large, dull brown/black patches that feel soft to the touch.
banana.action: Major defect if bruising exceeds 6 cm² per cluster.

banana.name: Crown Rot
banana.cause: Fungal complex infecting the cut surface of the banana hand during packing.
banana.symptoms: Blackening and softening of the crown tissue, often with white or grey mold growth.
banana.photo_guide: Look at the 'neck' where the bananas join. White fuzzy growth is a clear indicator.
banana.action: Any visible mold or rot on the crown is classified as a major defect.

avocado.name: Chilling Injury
avocado.cause: Storage at temperatures below 5°C (41°F) for extended periods.
avocado.symptoms: Greyish-brown skin discoloration (scald), surface pitting, and internal browning of the flesh.
avocado.photo_guide: The skin looks 'dusty' or greyish rather than a rich green or black. Internal flesh will have grey streaks.
avocado.action: High risk of uneven ripening; monitor closely.

avocado.name: Lenticel Damage
avocado.cause: Physiological response to water absorption (rain/soaking) combined with mechanical stress.
avocado.symptoms: Small, pinpoint brown or black spots centered on the fruit's breathing pores (lenticels).
avocado.photo_guide: Hundreds of tiny, uniform dark dots across the skin. Often mistaken for a disease.
avocado.action: Purely cosmetic if limited to the skin; does not affect eating quality.
